description: Alerting rules for the GCP Data & Analytics Pipeline chained event scenario

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Data Pipeline — High Failure Rate
rules.investigationGuide:
# High Failure Rate

The orchestrator is logging more failures than normal.

## Triage
1. Are failures on one DAG? Run: `FROM logs-gcp.composer-* | WHERE event.outcome == "failure" | STATS failures = COUNT(*) BY gcp.composer.dag_id | SORT failures DESC | LIMIT 10`
2. Check linked dashboard for the spike shape
3. Check if Dataproc/Spark or GCS rules also fired

## Escalate when
- Multiple DAGs failing
- Failures still rising after 15 min
- SLA-bound data affected

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Data Pipeline — Null/Empty Data Detected
rules.investigationGuide:
# Null/Empty Data Detected

A query returned zero rows — the upstream load may have silently failed.

## Triage
1. Identify the empty query: `FROM logs-gcp.bigquery-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND gcp.bigquery.rows_returned == 0 | KEEP @timestamp, gcp.bigquery.job_id, gcp.bigquery.dataset | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Check the source partition exists
3. If orchestrator shows "success" but query is empty — silent data loss

## Escalate when
- Query feeds an SLA-bound dataset
- Empty for two consecutive runs

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Data Pipeline — Dataproc/Spark Processing Error
rules.investigationGuide:
# Dataproc/Spark Processing Error

The compute cluster is logging exceptions.

## Triage
1. Classify errors: `FROM logs-gcp.dataproc-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND error.type IS NOT NULL | STATS errors = COUNT(*) BY error.type, host.id | SORT errors DESC | LIMIT 20`
2. Same host failing = infra; many hosts same error = code/data
3. Check if High Failure Rate also fired

## Escalate when
- Multiple clusters affected
- Same error after cluster restart

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Data Pipeline — GCS Source File Format Error
rules.investigationGuide:
# GCS Source File Format Error

An unexpected file format landed in the source bucket.

## Triage
1. List bad files: `FROM logs-gcp.gcs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h | WHERE NOT ENDS_WITH(gcp.gcs.object_name, ".avro") OR gcp.gcs.object_name LIKE "*%*" | KEEP @timestamp, gcp.gcs.object_name, gcp.gcs.bucket_name | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 50`
2. Check if one producer or many
3. Move bad file to quarantine prefix

## Escalate when
- Multiple producers writing wrong format
- Bucket versioning overwritten

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Data Pipeline — Slow Pipeline Run (>60s)
rules.investigationGuide:
# Slow Pipeline Run (>60s)

A DAG exceeded its informal SLA.

## Triage
1. Compare to baseline: `FROM logs-gcp.composer-* | WHERE event.action == "dag_completed" AND @timestamp > NOW() - 7d | STATS p50 = PERCENTILE(gcp.composer.duration_ms, 50), p95 = PERCENTILE(gcp.composer.duration_ms, 95) BY gcp.composer.dag_id | WHERE p95 > 30000 | SORT p95 DESC`
2. If within 2x of p95, likely noise
3. Check Dataproc errors and input volume

## Escalate when
- SLA-bound DAG slow for two consecutive runs
- Correlated with executor errors
